title: In Paris, in Tokyo, in Singapore, in Prague, I Stumble Out, Insane with Coffee
description: “In the pen barrel was a picture of a ferryboat, which sailed to the other end if you tilted the pen, turning the Mississippi into Niagara Falls.”
hook: A Paris Review story about a pen with a ferryboat inside—the kind of whimsical, precise observation that makes you feel less alone in your own strangeness.

title: Hagitude author Sharon Blackie: ‘At 60 I wasn’t ready to give up, I was just starting’
description:
The writer of cult hit If Women Rose Rooted is on a mission to bring folklore to modern readers. She talks about confronting her fears, communing with nature – and the power that comes with age

Like many of the wise women in her books, Sharon Blackie lives miles from anyone. Hers is the only house on the road winding through a valley deep in the Yorkshire Dales. The River Eden runs along the bottom of her garden, which overlooks the ruins of a castle built, as legend has it, b
hook: Sharon Blackie on refusing invisibility at 60, bringing folklore to readers who thought they'd aged out of wonder. A late-bloomer manifesto disguised as a profile.

title: Something Like Magic, Something Like Death: Thirst by John Robins
description:
Comedian and podcaster John Robins’ Thirst: Twelve Drinks That Changed My Life is an addiction memoir that's full to the brim with humour and humanity. It is, Simon Price finds out, quite the ride

Around three minutes into John Robins’ 2017 stand-up show The Darkness Of Robins, he recalls hitting his then all-time low. After a heavy ni
hook: John Robins turns his addiction memoir into a reckoning—twelve drinks, twelve reasons he almost died, all written with the dark wit that makes devastation bearable.

title: What Lionel Messi Sees
description: The World Cup’s all-time leading goal scorer is always paying attention.
hook: What does Messi see on the field that the rest of us miss? A deep dive into obsessive attention as a form of genius, not just athleticism.

title: Spoiler! How prediction markets broke reality TV.
description: We live in an era where you can bet on anything: sports, elections, wars, the temperature in Los Angeles tomorrow, company profits, and everything in between.  It shouldn’t be surprising, then, that people are spending millions of dollars on online prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket to bet on reality TV. Right now, you could place […]
hook: Prediction markets broke reality TV by turning suspense into a tradeable commodity—what happens when you can bet against the narrative?

title: The Voice of Google
description: I rose at Google by amplifying the company’s lofty rhetoric of humane innovation. Then I used my marketing skills as a tool for internal dissent.
hook: She built Google's marketing machine selling innovation, then weaponized those same skills to dissent from inside. A portrait of complicity and resistance.

title: The Lever
description: Every vortex closes to a single point.
hook: The Baffler's mysterious piece—just a title and the line 'Every vortex closes to a single point.' Sometimes the most intriguing writing is what it doesn't explain.
